# Module Specification: `AXI4XBar`

> **File:** `ts/src/modules/AXI4XBar/AXI4XBar.ts`
> **Status:** Draft

---

## Overview

**AXI4 Crossbar** — connects multiple AXI4 master interfaces to multiple AXI4 slave interfaces with address-based routing. The routing logic is generated by the external `rocket-chip-component-gen` tool (invoked via shell at elaboration time); TSSV wraps the generated SV file and wires up the AXI4 interface bundles. Each master gets an `inward` AXI4 interface; each slave gets an `outward` AXI4 interface.

> **Note:** Requires `third-party/rocket-chip-component-gen/` and a working Docker or native Rocket Chip build environment. The generated SV file is included as an `addSystemVerilogSubmodule`.

---

## Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `name` | `string` | Instance name (also used to name the inner generated module) |
| `masters` | `Array<{ name: string, id: [number, number] }>` | List of master interfaces. `id` is the `[min, max]` AXI ID range for that master. |
| `beatBytes` | `4 \| 8 \| 16 \| 32 \| 64 \| 128` | Bus data width in bytes (`beatBytes * 8` = data bits) |
| `addrBits` | `IntRange<16,64>` | Address bus width |
| `idBits` | `IntRange<1,16>` | AXI ID field width |
| `slaves` | `Array<{ name, address: { base, mask }, transferSizesWrite, transferSizesRead }>` | List of slave interfaces with address decode ranges and supported transfer sizes |

---

## IO Ports

| Port | Direction | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `clock` | input | `posedge` clock |
| `reset` | input | Active-high synchronous reset |

Each entry in `masters` generates an AXI4 `inward` interface bundle (named after `master.name`). Each entry in `slaves` generates an AXI4 `outward` interface bundle (named after `slave.name`). These are TSSV `Interface` objects, not flat ports — they appear as `AXI4 <name>()` in the generated SV.

---

## Functional Description

1. At TypeScript elaboration time, `params` are serialized to JSON and passed to `gen_rocket_component.sh AXI4XBar <json>` via `exec`. This invokes Rocket Chip's Scala generator and produces `componentgen/compile.dest/<name>_inner.sv`.
2. The TSSV wrapper declares all AXI4 master (`inward`) and slave (`outward`) interfaces using `addInterface`.
3. Signal bindings map between TSSV interface signal names (`AWVALID`, `WREADY`, etc.) and the generated SV's flat port names (e.g. `mastername_aw_valid`, `slavename_b_bits_id`) via a regex transform on the AXI channel prefix.
4. `addSystemVerilogSubmodule` instantiates the generated file.

### Address routing

Slave address ranges are defined by `{ base, mask }` — a transaction is routed to a slave when `(addr & ~mask) == base`. Gaps in address space produce no-response behavior (defined by Rocket Chip's interconnect).

---

## Dependencies

| Import | Source |
|---|---|
| `TSSV` (default) | `tssv/lib/core/TSSV` |
| `AXI4`, `AXI4_inward`, `AXI4_outward` | `tssv/lib/interfaces/AMBA/AMBA4/AXI4/r0p0_0/AXI4` |
| `exec` | Node.js `child_process` |
| `fs`, `os`, `path` | Node.js standard library |
| Rocket Chip component gen | `third-party/rocket-chip-component-gen/` |

---

## Test Plan

| Test case | Masters | Slaves | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Single master, single slave | 1 | 1 | Minimal smoke test |
| Multi-master arbitration | 2 | 1 | Concurrent access, verify no data corruption |
| Address decode | 1 | 2 | Transactions routed to correct slave by address |

```bash
npx tsc && node out/test/test_AXI4XBar.js   # requires rocket-chip env
```

> Verilator simulation requires the generated inner SV file to be present in `third-party/rocket-chip-component-gen/componentgen/compile.dest/`.
